item.title: SSL/TLS
item.description: SSL/TLS is a security protocol used to protect data sent over the internet.

item.title: WAS (Web Application Server)
item.description: A WAS is the server that runs the main business logic of a web application. A Spring Boot backend server can be considered a WAS.

item.title: CIA Triad
item.description: The CIA Triad is a basic cybersecurity model used to protect information.

item.title: CompTIA Security+
item.description: Main Areas Security Fundamentals Cybersecurity CIA Triad Defense in Depth Zero Trust Threats and Vulnerabilities Vulnerability Management Social Engineering Risk and Governance Risk Management Cryptography and Network Security Cryptography SSL and TLS Computer Network .

item.title: Computer Network
item.description: A computer network is a group of connected devices that can communicate and share data with each other.

item.title: Cryptography
item.description: Cryptography is the practice of protecting information by converting it into a secure form.

item.title: Cybersecurity
item.description: Cybersecurity is about understanding how systems break, how people make mistakes, and how to protect information from threats.

item.title: Defense in Depth
item.description: Defense in Depth is a cybersecurity strategy that uses multiple layers of protection.
